step_by_step_thinking:
Hard swelling at the angle of mandible with numerous draining sinuses strongly points towards **Mucormycosis** due to its characteristic presentation involving facial cellulitis leading to orbital involvement and necrotic changes around the maxillofacial area, often accompanied by sinus tract formation.
\boxed{C} Based on the description of hard swelling at the angle of mandible with numerous draining sinuses, along with the clinical characteristics typically seen in Mucormycosis, option C stands out as the most probable diagnosis given the rapid progression and severe local spread indicative of this fungal infection.
